title: Disability Evaluation and the Use of Psychological Tests (IOM, NBK305232)
description: Chapter 2 of the Institute of Medicine report 'Psychological Testing in the Service of Disability Determination' (National Academies Press, 2015), describing SSA's process for evaluating SSDI and SSI applications and the role of psychological and validity testing in disability evaluations. Covers application volumes, base rates of malingering, standardized data collection, and comparisons with non-SSA systems (military, VA). Relevant to DDS examiners and medical/psychological consultants weighing psychological-test evidence.

source.publisher: National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI) Bookshelf / Institute of Medicine, National Academies Press (US)
source.title: Psychological Testing in the Service of Disability Determination — Chapter 2: Disability Evaluation and the Use of Psychological Tests

license: Public — National Academies Press / IOM consensus report

Markdown scrape retaining NCBI links; no tabular columns to declare.
